notes: These envelopes are local deterministic release guardrails, not fork/testnet/mainnet gas evidence.
The exact Foundry snapshot remains the drift detector; these ceilings document maximum accepted release budgets for high-risk user flows.

Ceilings are rounded operational envelopes, usually around 10-20% above the committed local measurement unless the path is intentionally heavy; increases should be investigated and justified before bumping a ceiling.

envelopes.rationale: First bid is a core collector action and must remain comfortably below 100k gas locally.
Outbid collectors must be able to withdraw credits through a compact pull-payment path.
Near-end outbids create bidder credit and extend the auction; this MEV-sensitive path should stay bounded.
Poster, curator, and protocol auction proceeds withdrawal must stay inexpensive for normal settlement operations.
No-bid settlement returns custody and closes the auction without payment accounting.
With-bid settlement transfers custody and allocates pull-payment credits across owed categories.
Curators must be able to withdraw earned credits through the pull-payment path.
Merkle reward claims are a recurring curator workflow and should stay bounded.
Dependency script reads are collector/indexer-facing metadata operations.
Final on-chain tokenURI rendering is intentionally heavy but must remain below the local one-million-gas envelope.
Poster and protocol fixed-price pull-payment withdrawals should stay inexpensive.
Fixed-price signed mint execution is a primary drop path and should stay within the documented envelope.
The launch mint manager max-batch path intentionally covers 10 prepared mints with 16 static counters each, and the ceiling documents that bounded administrative/executor path.
